description: Shared Renovate preset for digitalnsw repos. Consumer repos extend this via the synced renovate.json (source: repo-files/renovate.json). Edit HERE; Renovate reads it from main at run time, so changes apply everywhere without a sync.

vulnerabilityAlerts.description: Security alerting/fix PRs stay with Snyk — avoids duplicate PRs for the same CVE.

schedule: before 7am on the first day of the month
lockFileMaintenance.description: Monthly full lockfile regeneration with real npm — keeps lockfiles fresh and valid.

packageRules.description: npm engine majors are blocked until the platform bundles the new major. engine-strict=true means bumping engines.npm ahead of the Vercel/CI bundled npm fails every install with EBADENGINE (see nswds-email#460 — npm 12 needs Node ^22.22.2 || ^24.15.0, expected bundled with Node 26 ~Oct 2026). Remove this rule as part of the coordinated adoption pass (reusable-ci + engines.node floor + lockfile regen, same shape as nswds-email#458).

In-range lockfile-only bumps of override-pinned packages are disabled: Renovate applies them via `npm install <pkg>@<version>`, which adds the package as a direct dependency whose spec conflicts with the override, so npm aborts with EOVERRIDE and the whole grouped PR ships a stale lockfile that fails npm ci everywhere (see nswds-email#459, fast-uri). Monthly lockFileMaintenance regenerates with a plain `npm install` and keeps these pins fresh, so nothing is lost.

conventional-changelog-conventionalcommits v10 is blocked: preset v10's render-function templates are incompatible with @semantic-release/release-notes-generator@14 — commit analysis still computes correct release types, but generateNotes silently emits an empty changelog body (reproduced with the shared release.config.mjs; tracked upstream as issue #992). Remove once a release compatible with release-notes-generator 14 ships. NOTE: v10 can also arrive transitively (@commitlint/config-conventional@21 depends on ccc ^10 and hoists it where the preset loader finds it — this blanked nswds-email changelogs 1.140.5–1.140.12 via nswds-email#437), so repos running semantic-release must also carry a direct v9 pin.

typescript majors (6/7) are blocked: the typescript@7 npm package is the native Go compiler and require('typescript') exposes no JS compiler API, so `next build` fails (Next 16.2.x: 'required package(s) not installed', broke CI and Vercel deploy), eslint crashes via @typescript-eslint/typescript-estree (peer range >=4.8.4 <6.1.0), and prettier-plugin-organize-imports silently no-ops — imports stop being sorted while format hooks keep passing (see nswds-email#444; reproduced 2026-07-18). Native `tsc -b` itself works. Remove when Next.js and typescript-eslint declare TS 6/7 support.

eslint majors are blocked: ESLint 10 removed context.getFilename(), which eslint-plugin-react 7.37.5 (pulled in transitively by eslint-config-next, incl. latest 16.2.10) still calls — every eslint invocation crashes with a TypeError before linting a single file, even on plain .ts files (reproduced on nswds-app#418; upstream jsx-eslint/eslint-plugin-react#3977, vercel/next.js#89764). PR CI does not run lint, so the breakage would merge green and only surface in local dev. Remove once eslint-config-next ships with an ESLint-10-compatible eslint-plugin-react (watch vercel/next.js#89764), and re-check the other capped peers (eslint-plugin-import, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y) at the same time.
